name: Pranav Gawai
college: MIT Art Design and Technology University, Pune
current_role: Tech Lead at early-stage AI SaaS startup (pre-funding, building MVP)
about: I am Pranav Gawai, a final-year CSE student at MITADT Pune. I have been building real products since second year. Currently Tech Lead at an AI SaaS startup where I architect the entire stack — LLM integrations, RAG pipelines, FastAPI backends. I build AI-powered products. I think in products, not just code.
strengths: I learn by building, not by studying. I shipped Travio in 7 days. I understand AI infrastructure deeply — memory layers, retrieval systems, production RAG pipelines. I take ownership.
weakness: DSA under time pressure. I recognised this and started a structured plan — Striver's sheet, one problem daily, pattern-based. Improving consistently.

projects.name: PlacePro
projects.what: Full-stack campus placement management platform for MITADT Pune
projects.stack: FastAPI, PostgreSQL, React, Redis, Docker, Railway, JWT, RLS
projects.impact: Manages 200+ student records. Proposed to TPO office for official adoption.
projects.hardest_part: PostgreSQL RLS for multi-tenant access — enforcing role-based data isolation at database layer not application layer
projects.scale: Would add WebSockets for real-time updates, horizontal scaling with Redis pub/sub

projects.name: Mnemo
projects.what: Persistent long-term memory layer for LLM assistants
projects.stack: Node.js, TypeScript, OpenAI, pgvector, PostgreSQL, RLS
projects.impact: Decay-scored memory — older memories lose relevance weight over time. RLS multi-user isolation.
projects.hardest_part: Designing the decay algorithm and making retrieval consider both semantic similarity and memory recency simultaneously
projects.differentiator: Unlike Mem0, uses decay scoring so memories age naturally

projects.name: Travio
projects.stack: Next.js 16, React 19, MongoDB, Clerk, Cloudinary, Vercel, Framer Motion
projects.impact: Live at travio.fun. Shipped in 7 days for Buildathon.
projects.hardest_part: RBAC with three roles (Owner/Editor/Viewer) enforced at both API and UI layer

projects.name: Detectra
projects.what: AI anomaly detection combining ML classification with LLM reasoning
projects.stack: Python, FastAPI, Scikit-learn, OpenAI API, PostgreSQL, Docker
projects.hardest_part: Combining Scikit-learn anomaly scores with OpenAI contextual reasoning in a single pipeline

projects.name: CraftaStudio
projects.what: AI code generation startup — Tech Lead role
projects.stack: LangChain, FastAPI, DAG engine using Kahn + Tarjan algorithms, multi-model fallback
projects.note: Confidential — early stage startup with professor

achievements: 1st Place — National Cybersecurity Hackathon, Healthcare Track (24 hours)
2nd Place — AI Grand Challenge, National Level (Fintech Track)
Top 10 out of 800+ Teams — Smart India Hackathon 2025 (built Sahara mental health AI)
MacMonium open source — macOS harmonium via lid-angle sensor — 7299 LinkedIn impressions
Placement Coordinator at MITADT Pune — manages company visits and student placement process

hr_answers.tell_me_about_yourself: My name is Pranav Gawai. I am a final-year CSE student specialising in AI and ML at MITADT Pune. I have been building and shipping real products since my second year. Currently I serve as Tech Lead at an early-stage AI SaaS startup where I architect the entire stack — LLM integrations, RAG pipelines, and FastAPI backends. Before that I built PlacePro, a campus placement management system proposed to my university's TPO office. I built Travio, a collaborative trip planning SaaS live at travio.fun. I won 1st place at a national Cybersecurity Hackathon and 2nd place at the AI Grand Challenge. I am looking for an AI Engineer or SDE role where I can build impactful products from day one.
hr_answers.strengths: My biggest strength is that I learn by building. I shipped Travio in 7 days from zero to live. I understand AI infrastructure deeply — not just calling APIs but designing memory and retrieval layers around them. I think in products, not just code, which is why PlacePro was proposed to our university TPO office.
hr_answers.weakness: Honestly, DSA problem solving under time pressure. I recognised this gap and started a structured plan — Striver's sheet, one problem every morning, pattern-based approach. I have improved from struggling with Easy problems to consistently solving Mediums. I treat it as a skill to train.
hr_answers.why_hire: Because I am already doing the job. I am Tech Lead at a startup at 20. PlacePro is live at my university. Travio is live at travio.fun. I won a national hackathon in 24 hours. Most candidates talk about what they will do. I can show you what I have already done.
hr_answers.five_years: In two years I want to be a strong senior AI or full stack engineer deep in a product I care about. In four years a technical lead or architect. In five years either building my own product or leading a technical team solving a genuinely hard problem.
